framework_selection.llm_interface: OpenAI-compatible API (for GPT-OSS-20B generator and judge)
framework_selection.config_management: YAML + dataclasses (no Hydra — keeps deps lightweight)
framework_selection.reasoning: Paper is a Python-ecosystem benchmark. No PyTorch training loop required — the embedding models are used inference-only via sentence-transformers. FAISS provides the cosine similarity index. LangChain text splitters directly implement two of the required chunkers. All four robust methods (Fixed-size, Recursive, HAC, Max-min) are implementable with these libraries.

files.description: Package init. Exports top-level symbols.
Chunker sub-package init. Exports ChunkerRegistry.
Abstract base class for all chunkers. Enforces the f_theta(D) -> C interface from EQ2.

BaseChunker: chunk(self, document: str) -> list[str]  # f_theta: D -> C

files.description: Fixed-size chunker: chunk_size=512 tokens, overlap=50. Wraps LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with character-count mode.
Recursive semantic chunker: LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with semantic separators hierarchy [paragraph, sentence, word]. Default config from LangChain.

RecursiveSemanticChunker: __init__(self, chunk_size: int = 512, overlap: int = 50, separators: list[str] | None = None) -> None

files.description: Sequential Hierarchical Agglomerative Chunking. Splits document into sentences, embeds each, merges adjacent pairs by cosine similarity until threshold not met. Enforces narrative continuity (no positional weight tuning).

SequentialHACChunker: __init__(self, similarity_threshold: float = 0.85, embed_model: str = 'BAAI/bge-m3', max_chunk_tokens: int = 512) -> None

files.sir_mapping: SIR: Sequential HAC Chunker — Qu et al. 2025
files.note: similarity_threshold default from original publication; flag as TODO verify

files.description: Max-Min Semantic Chunking. Greedy: adds sentences to current chunk if max similarity to chunk exceeds adaptive threshold derived from chunk's minimum coherence. Controls chunk growth dynamically.

MaxMinChunker: _should_extend(self, sentence_emb: np.ndarray, chunk_embeddings: np.ndarray, threshold: float) -> bool

files.sir_mapping: SIR: Max-Min Semantic Chunking — Kiss et al. 2025
files.note: alpha parameter controls threshold sensitivity; default from publication — TODO verify

files.description: TextTiling (Hearst 1997) with paper-specified modification: chunk boundaries aligned to nearest SENTENCE (not paragraph). Uses lexical cohesion / block comparison to detect topic shifts.

TextTilingChunker: __init__(self, w: int = 20, k: int = 10, smoothing_width: int = 2) -> None

files.description: Embedding module. Wraps sentence-transformers for bge-m3. Provides batch encode for chunks and queries. Returns L2-normalized vectors for cosine similarity.

ChunkEmbedder: __init__(self, model_name: str = 'BAAI/bge-m3', device: str = 'auto', batch_size: int = 64) -> None
encode(self, texts: list[str], normalize: bool = True) -> np.ndarray  # [N, d=1024]
encode_query(self, query: str, normalize: bool = True) -> np.ndarray  # [d=1024]

files.sir_mapping: SIR: Embedding Model (bge-m3); EQ3 — E(q), E(c)

files.description: Reranker module. Wraps bge-reranker-v2-m3 cross-encoder to re-score retrieved chunks.

ChunkReranker: rerank(self, query: str, chunks: list[str], top_k: int = 5) -> list[tuple[str, float]]  # (chunk, score) sorted desc

files.description: FAISS vector index. Stores (chunk_text, embedding) pairs. Implements EQ3: top-k cosine retrieval.

FAISSChunkIndex: __init__(self, embedding_dim: int = 1024, use_gpu: bool = False) -> None
build(self, chunks: list[str], embeddings: np.ndarray) -> None
search(self, query_emb: np.ndarray, k: int = 10) -> tuple[list[str], list[float]]  # chunks, scores

files.description: End-to-end retrieval pipeline: embeds query, searches index, reranks. Returns top-k chunks for downstream use.

RAGRetriever: __init__(self, index: FAISSChunkIndex, embedder: ChunkEmbedder, reranker: ChunkReranker | None = None) -> None
retrieve(self, query: str, k: int = 10) -> list[str]
retrieve_with_scores(self, query: str, k: int = 10) -> list[tuple[str, float]]

files.description: LLM generation wrapper. Calls OpenAI-compatible API with query + top-5 chunks as context (max 4000 tokens). Used for Experiment 2.

RAGGenerator: __init__(self, model: str = 'gpt-oss-20b', api_base: str, api_key: str, max_context_tokens: int = 4000) -> None
generate(self, query: str, context_chunks: list[str]) -> str

files.sir_mapping: SIR: Generator LLM (GPT-OSS-20B, top-5 chunks, 4000 token limit)

files.description: LLM-as-a-Judge module. Submits (query, generated_answer, ground_truth) to GPT-OSS-20B and parses 1–5 Likert score. Used for Experiment 2 evaluation.

LLMJudge: __init__(self, model: str = 'gpt-oss-20b', api_base: str, api_key: str) -> None
score(self, query: str, answer: str, ground_truth: str) -> int  # 1-5

files.description: Metric computation. Implements EQ4 (relevance label), EQ5 (Accuracy@k), EQ6 (Recall@k) exactly as defined in the paper.

RetrievalMetrics: accuracy_at_k(retrieved_chunks: list[str], relevant_chunks: list[str], k: int) -> float  # EQ5
recall_at_k(retrieved_chunks: list[str], relevant_chunks: list[str], k: int) -> float   # EQ6
is_relevant(chunk: str, answer_span: str) -> bool  # EQ4 — overlap check
compute_all(results: list[dict], k_acc: int = 5, k_rec: int = 10) -> dict

files.description: Aggregates per-query results into per-method, per-dataset tables. Outputs CSV and JSON matching Tables 1, 2, 3, 4 from the paper. Also logs chunking time.

ResultReporter: record(self, method: str, dataset: str, metric: str, value: float) -> None
record_timing(self, method: str, dataset: str, seconds: float) -> None

files.description: Dataset loading utilities. Downloads/loads each of the 11 dataset configurations (including merged stress-test variants). Returns standardized (document, query, answer_span) records.

DatasetLoader: create_merged_variant(self, documents: list[str]) -> str  # single concatenated doc

files.description: Orchestrates the full chunking → embedding → indexing sequence for one (method, dataset) pair. Enforces the 48-hour timeout. Catches and logs T/S failure markers.

ChunkingPipeline: __init__(self, chunker: BaseChunker, embedder: ChunkEmbedder, timeout_hours: float = 48.0) -> None
run(self, documents: list[str]) -> tuple[FAISSChunkIndex, list[list[str]], float]  # index, all_chunks, elapsed_s

files.sir_mapping: SIR: 48h time limit enforcement; T/S failure markers

files.description: Orchestrates Experiment 1 (retrieval) and Experiment 2 (end-to-end generation) for a given (chunker, dataset) pair. Calls metrics and reporter.

EvalPipeline: __init__(self, retriever: RAGRetriever, metrics: RetrievalMetrics, reporter: ResultReporter, generator: RAGGenerator | None = None, judge: LLMJudge | None = None) -> None
run_retrieval_eval(self, queries: list[dict], method_name: str, dataset_name: str) -> dict
run_generation_eval(self, queries: list[dict], method_name: str, dataset_name: str) -> dict

files.description: Config dataclasses and YAML loader. Parses config.yaml into typed Python objects.
Context manager and decorator for measuring chunking wall-clock time. Used by ChunkingPipeline.
files.sir_mapping: SIR: Table 3 — chunking time per method

files.description: Shared NLP utilities: sentence splitting (NLTK punkt), token counting (tiktoken), answer span overlap check.
files.sir_mapping: SIR EQ4 overlap, TextTiling sentence alignment

pipeline_A_chunking_and_indexing.name: Chunking + Indexing Pass
pipeline_A_chunking_and_indexing.description: Offline pass: convert documents to a searchable index
steps: INPUT: documents: list[str]  ← raw text from DatasetLoader
chunks: list[str] = chunker.chunk(doc)     ← EQ2: f_theta(D) -> C

pipeline_B_retrieval_eval.name: Experiment 1: Evidence Retrieval
steps: INPUT: query_record: dict {query: str, answer_span: str, relevant_doc_id: str}
candidates: list[str], scores: list[float] = index.search(query_emb, k=10)  ← EQ3

pipeline_C_generation_eval.name: Experiment 2: End-to-End RAG
steps: score: int = judge.score(query, answer, ground_truth_answer)  ← 1-5 Likert

recursive_semantic.note: # Default LangChain separators — TODO verify exact config from paper

sequential_hac.note: # ASSUMED: threshold from Qu et al. 2025 default — TODO verify

max_min.note: # ASSUMED: alpha from Kiss et al. 2025 default — TODO verify

texttiling.note: # sentence alignment explicitly stated in paper Section 3

embedding.note: # ASSUMED: dim=1024 from bge-m3 spec; normalize=True for cosine

rationale.nltk: Sentence tokenization (punkt) for HAC, Max-min, TextTiling
rationale.spacy: Sentence splitting fallback; TextTiling uses it (also source of S-marker bug)
rationale.datasets: HuggingFace datasets for SQuAD, TriviaQA, NQ, Qasper loading

environment_yaml.name: rag-chunking-bench
environment_yaml.note: Use faiss-gpu instead of faiss-cpu if CUDA available

chunk.py.description: Run chunking only for a given method+dataset. Saves chunks to disk and logs timing.

index.py.description: Build FAISS index from pre-computed chunks.

evaluate.py.description: Run Experiment 1 (retrieval eval: Accuracy@5, Recall@10) given an existing index.

generate_and_judge.py.description: Run Experiment 2 (end-to-end RAG + LLM judge). Requires OPENAI_API_KEY env var.

run_full_bench.py.description: Master entrypoint. Runs all stages (chunk→index→eval1→eval2) for all enabled methods and datasets. Respects 48h timeout per combination. Outputs Tables 1-4.

report.py.description: Aggregate raw results JSONs into human-readable tables and plots matching the paper.

docker_spec.note: For GPU support, switch base to nvidia/cuda:12.1.0-runtime-ubuntu22.04 and install faiss-gpu

risk_assessment.description: GutenQA, LiteraryQA, NovelQA are non-standard datasets not on HuggingFace Hub. GutenQA is from Duarte et al. 2024 (EMNLP), LiteraryQA from Bonomo et al. 2025, NovelQA from Wang et al. 2025. Download scripts must point to ACL Anthology or GitHub releases.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide download_datasets.py with dataset-specific loaders and fallback instructions. Add smoke-test mode with SQuAD only (available via HuggingFace).

risk_assessment.description: GPT-OSS-20B is referenced as the generator and judge model. This is a non-standard model name — likely an internal/OpenAI-hosted open-source model. Users may not have access to this exact model.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Abstract generator and judge behind an OpenAI-compatible interface. Config allows substituting any model (gpt-4o, claude, llama via vLLM). Add warning in README: exact reproduction requires GPT-OSS-20B access.

risk_assessment.description: SequentialHACChunker similarity_threshold (0.85) is assumed from publication defaults but not explicitly stated in this paper (Section 3 says 'default hyperparameters from original publications'). Wrong threshold significantly changes chunk granularity.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Mark as configurable in config.yaml with a clear ASSUMED comment. Expose as CLI arg. Document that users should cross-reference Qu et al. 2025.

risk_assessment.description: MaxMinChunker's alpha parameter is assumed from Kiss et al. 2025. If the publication uses a different default, chunking behavior and results will diverge.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Same as RISK-03: configurable + ASSUMED comment + cross-reference.

risk_assessment.description: Reproducing the S (spaCy memory error) behavior on documents > 1M chars is intentional for the benchmark but may silently succeed on machines with more RAM, giving different results from the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Log document length. Add optional --force_spacy_limit flag that artificially caps memory for fair comparison. Document this in README.

risk_assessment.description: The paper uses PIRB as the evaluation protocol (accuracy/recall framework), but PIRB is a Polish IR benchmark. The relationship between PIRB's eval harness and the non-Polish datasets (SQuAD, TriviaQA) is ambiguous (SIR ambiguity: confidence 0.70).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement accuracy_at_k and recall_at_k as standalone metric functions (EQ5, EQ6) independent of PIRB's tooling. This is faithful to the math regardless of the evaluation framework used.

risk_assessment.description: Paper runs on H100 96GB GPUs with 1006GB RAM. Most users will have significantly less compute, affecting runtime but not correctness. The 48h timeout may trigger differently.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement resumable pipeline (--resume flag). Log elapsed time prominently. Adjust timeout via config.

risk_assessment.description: These three methods are not part of the core robust-methods set. Their original implementations require additional dependencies (LLM API, spaCy graph ops). Partial reproduction is expected.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Place in chunkers/experimental/ subfolder. Document expected T/S failures per the paper. Include stub classes that raise NotImplementedError with paper citation.
